preview.block:
<article_type_context type="original_research" display="Original research (full IMRAD)">
  DEEP_STRUCTURE (content from user data only; patterns are cadence templates):
    ABSTRACT_FORMAT: mandatory labeled sub-paragraphs: Background / Methods / Results / Conclusion (each on its own line with label + colon).
    No inline citations in Background or Conclusion.
    End Conclusion with mechanistic insight: State what the data imply beyond platform utility (e.g. pathway X occurs independently of Y)..
    Pattern — Background: 2 sentences: disease/gap + why in vivo human innate immunity is needed.
    Pattern — Methods: 1-2 sentences: model + challenge design only.
    Pattern — Results: 3-4 sentences: engraftment + pathway readouts + pharmacology with numbers.
    Pattern — Conclusion: 1-2 sentences: platform competence + one mechanistic insight.
    ABSTRACT_LENGTH: 200–350 words.
  SECTION_RULES section="abstract":
    SECTION_WORD_TARGET_DEFAULT: 280 words (override if user sets section_word_target).
</article_type_context>

Deep structure rules in <article_type_context> define WHAT to say in each section. Journal rules in <journal_context> define citation format and forbidden phrases. Never copy exemplar sentences verbatim — adapt patterns to user-supplied facts only.

abstract.text: Dysregulated innate immune responses drive inflammatory diseases, yet clinical translation of mouse innate immunity research remains limited by species differences in immune signaling. Studying human inflammatory reactions in vivo is challenging due to inadequate human myeloid cell engraftment in existing humanized mouse models. Here we show that NSG-QUAD mice expressing four human myelopoiesis transgenes (IL-3, GM-CSF, SCF, CSF1) reconstituted with human CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells develop functional human innate immune cells capable of mounting NF-κB, type I interferon, and NLRP3 inflammasome responses to lipopolysaccharide challenge. Human monocytes, dendritic cells, and granulocytes engrafted in peripheral blood, spleen, and liver by 6 weeks post-injection. Humanized NSG-QUAD mice responded to intraperitoneal and intranasal LPS with production of NF-κB-dependent cytokines including TNF-α, IL-6, IL-12p70, and IL-8, as well as type I interferon. LPS challenge induced NLRP3 inflammasome-mediated IL-1β and IL-18 secretion in serum and tissues. Pharmacological validation demonstrated that MCC950 specifically abrogated IL-1β and IL-18 production without affecting NF-κB-dependent cytokines or human monocyte viability, confirming that inflammasome activation occurs independently of pyroptotic cell death. These data demonstrate that humanized NSG-QUAD mice provide a competent in vivo platform for studying NF-κB, type I interferon, and inflammasome effectors of human innate immunity, enabling preclinical evaluation of human-specific innate immune therapeutics.
